Corresponding Author: O. Wachsen; K. H. Reichert; R. P. Krüger; H. Much; G. Schulz
Physical Address: Technical University of Berlin, Institute of Technical Chemistry, Sekr. TC 3, Str. d. 17. Juni 135, D-10623 Berlin, Germany; Institute of Applied Chemistry, Berlin-Adlershof e. V. (ACA), Rudower Chaussee 5, D-12484 Berlin, Germany
Critical Condition Basis: explicit mention of critical conditions for poly-L-lactide
Detector: Diode-Array-Detector (type UVD 3205, Gynkotek, Germering, Germany) and a UV-detector (type SPD-10A, Shimadzu Europa GmbH, Duisburg, Germany)
Evidence Text: The 'critical' conditions for poly-L-lactide were realised by a mixture of 60vol.% dioxane and 40vol.% n-hexane which was used as solvent and mobile phase. A Nucleosil 300-S column (250mm 4mm ID, Macharey-Nagel, Diuren, Germany) was used. The column was thermostated at 30°C.
Notes: Used for LACCC analysis of thermal degradation products.

Physical Address: Department of Polymer Chemistry, Center of Molecular and Macromolecular Studies, Polish Academy of Sciences, Sienkiewicza 112, PL-90-363 Lodz, Poland; Deutsches Kunststoff-Institut (German Institute for Polymers), Schlossgartenstrasse 6, D-64289 Darmstadt, Germany
Critical Condition Basis: elution volumes independent of molar mass
Detector: Waters 486 tunable UV detector operating at 235 nm
Evidence Text: At the critical point of adsorption, a series of linear C4H9-PLA-OH's having molar masses (Mn) in the range from 2.3 £ 10[3] to 7.4 £ 10[4] ... showed no dependence of the elution volumes on molar mass. ... LC-CC conditions have been used corresponding to silica gel as the stationary phase and a mixture of 1,4-dioxane/n-hexane (56.25/43.75 by vol%) at 50 8C as the mobile phase.
Notes: Critical conditions established for linear PLA used to analyze star-shaped PLA.

Physical Address: Department of Polymer Chemistry, Center of Molecular and Macromolecular Studies, Polish Academy of Sciences, Sienkiewicza 112, PL-90-363 Lodz, Poland; Deutsches Kunststoff-Institut (German Institute for Polymers), Schlossgartenstrasse 6, D-64289 Darmstadt, Germany
Critical Condition Basis: elution volume independent of molar mass (established on linear analog)
Evidence Text: The series of starshaped PLA's have been analyzed by LC-CC ... with regard to possible structural imperfections. ... Measurements were carried out at 50 8C using the critical compositions that have been established for linear PLA (i.e. 1,4-dioxane/n-hexane 56.25: 43.75 vol%).
Notes: Analysis of star-shaped PLA performed under critical conditions established for linear PLA. Separation based on number of arms (end-groups).

Physical Address: Deutsches Kunststoff-Institut (German Institute for Polymers), Schlossgartenstr. 6, 64289 Darmstadt, Germany; Department of Polymer Chemistry, Center of Molecular and Macromolecular Studies, Polish Academy of Sciences, Sienkiewicza 112, PL-90-363 Lodz, Poland
Critical Condition Basis: critical conditions determined for linear PLAs
Evidence Text: The critical conditions for linear PLAs with molar masses ranging from 4800 to 26000 g/mol were determined at 50 8C at a composition of 57.1/42.9 (1,4-dioxane/nhexane). Despite the different molar masses, all samples elute at the same elution volume (Fig. 1).
Notes: Conditions established for linear PLA and applied to star-shaped PLAs.
